mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive SPX returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.10 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases correlates negatively with 5-day SPX returns, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained wage income expansion and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 200, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or Rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable federal funds rates signals a soft landing environment that supports corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS stability fails to predict SPX direction with a hit rate above 50 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below 95, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.
